default.answer: Here's where you stand for Backend Developer: you're at 40/100 with JavaScript and Git verified, but missing Node.js, Express.js, REST APIs and MongoDB. Your fastest move is to start Node.js — it's Week 1-4 of your roadmap and unlocks everything after it.

answers.answer: I'm your CareerPilot mentor, so I only speak to what's in your analysis — I won't make things up outside it. Let's stay on your Backend Developer goal: your fastest next step is starting Node.js, your #1 missing required skill (Week 1-4 of your roadmap).

For Backend Developer you're at 40/100, so not for backend internships yet — you're missing Node.js, Express.js, REST APIs and MongoDB. You could apply to general or frontend-leaning internships now (you have JavaScript and Git), but close the backend gaps first to be competitive.

answers.answer: Your highest-priority gap is Node.js — it's Week 1-4 of your roadmap. Build a Node.js backend API to start; it's the foundation for Express, REST APIs and everything else on your plan.

answers.answer: Build a REST API for a notes or task app using Node.js and Express. It's your first roadmap milestone's project and closes your Node.js gap directly — then extend it with MongoDB to knock out another gap.

answers.answer: Realistically, yes. You already have JavaScript and Git. Your roadmap closes the four missing skills over 16 weeks: Node.js (1-4), Express.js (5-8), REST APIs (9-12), MongoDB (13-16). Do those plus one portfolio API and your readiness should climb from 40 toward the 80s.

For your Backend Developer target, focus on Node.js — it's your #1 missing required skill. React isn't required for this role, so it won't raise your readiness. If you later switch to Full Stack, we'd add React then.

answers.answer: Certifications aren't on your roadmap and won't change your readiness the way closing skill gaps will. Your time is better spent building the Node.js → Express → REST → MongoDB projects on your plan; a shipped project beats a cert for entry-level backend roles in your data.

answers.answer: Week 1 = your first roadmap milestone: start Node.js. Concretely — follow the Node.js docs plus a Node crash course, then build a tiny Express server with one route. That single step begins closing your top gap and sets up next week's REST API work.
